summary: local-mcp ships install/postinstall code that beacons system data to a Railway-hosted C2
details: The package contains lifecycle and import-time code (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that loads child_process, os, fs, http, and https, reads process.env and platform/host fields, and issues HTTP/HTTPS requests to a hardcoded endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app. The same files also reference https://download.local-mcp.com and https://local-mcp.com, but the Railway-hosted host is the operative C2 destination — a freshly-provisioned PaaS host bearing no relationship to a legitimate vendor SDK CDN. The combination of child_process + os + https + hardcoded non-publisher endpoint + env-var/system-info collection inside install/postinstall scripts (setup.js shows multiple POST call sites) matches the install-time exfiltration / dropper fingerprint. Installing this package causes outbound system metadata to be sent to attacker-controlled infrastructure and likely fetches/executes additional payload via the download script.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
